context: Tests whether the App Intents skill guidance is applied to widget configuration, Control Center controls, SnippetIntent, and sibling boundary routing.

checklist.name: Widget versus control protocols
checklist.description: Separates `WidgetConfigurationIntent` for configurable widgets from `ControlConfigurationIntent` for Control Center controls, and does not reuse one widget configuration intent for all surfaces.

checklist.name: Configuration intent behavior
checklist.description: Treats configuration intents primarily as configuration contracts and avoids putting shared action behavior in a `perform()` method on the configuration intent unless there is a deliberate separate action use.

checklist.name: Control preview optionality
checklist.description: States that `ControlConfigurationIntent` parameters without defaults must be optional so Control Center preview/configuration can instantiate the control.

checklist.name: Shared action intent
checklist.description: Moves accessory state changes into a separate action intent such as an `AppIntent` or `SetValueIntent` with explicit entity parameters, rather than mutating from widget configuration or snippet rendering.

checklist.name: Snippet side effects
checklist.description: Explains that `SnippetIntent.perform()` may be called multiple times and should remain side-effect-free, with accessory mutations performed before returning the snippet or in a separate action intent.

checklist.name: Snippet result shape
checklist.description: Describes the caller returning a snippet, such as `.result(snippetIntent:)`, and the snippet intent returning a view-oriented result such as `ShowsSnippetView`.

checklist.name: WidgetKit boundary
checklist.description: Keeps WidgetKit timeline provider and layout rewrite details out of the App Intents plan except for the protocol boundary needed to supply widget configuration.

checklist.name: Sensitive action handling
checklist.description: Mentions appropriate authentication, confirmation, or authorization review for smart-home state changes from system surfaces.
